title: The Man Swollen with Dropsy
subtitle: Christ heals the dropsical man on the Sabbath, and calls the proud to the lowest place

stillness: Be still, and withdraw a little from the noise of the table.
Sometimes we must be drawn away from the quiet of contemplation for the sake of souls.
Let your heart grow silent before the One who knows the thoughts of men.
gospel: Jesus went through the cities and villages,
teaching, and preaching without favor, not regarding persons.
And it came to pass, when he entered the house of a certain ruler of the Pharisees on the Sabbath,
that in his deeds they might find something to reproach and accuse him.
And behold, a certain man who had the dropsy was before him,
hoping for a remedy for his infirmity.
And Jesus, answering, said to the Pharisees and the lawyers:
Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?
And he took hold of him, and healed him by touching,
and let him go freely, without price.
behold: Behold the Lord Jesus entering the Pharisee's house to eat bread on the Sabbath,
not for the food, but for the gain of souls.
Mark the great malice of those who fed him and laid snares for him:
they ate with him, and wished to bite him.
And see the man swollen with dropsy standing before him,
who, from fear of the Pharisees, did not ask for a remedy,
but only stood there, that being seen, Christ might have mercy and heal him.
And the Lord, knowing this, did not ask whether he wished to be made whole,
He touched him, not from necessity, but from humility;
and that he might show his divinity in the flesh, he made him whole.
consider: Note that the properties of the dropsical man are seven, and by them the seven capital sins are designated;
and so this dropsical man is said to signify every sinner.
The dropsical man has first the swelling of the body: so the proud man, the inflation of the heart.
He has the thirst's burning: so the covetous man — the more he drinks of riches, the more he thirsts.
For the love of money grows as much as the money itself grows.
Covetousness is a most savage plague, which makes beggars of those it seizes,
for it finds no end of its seeking, and the more it is enriched, the more it begs.
And this dropsy especially signifies pride:
as in the dropsical man there was the swelling inflation of the body, so in the proud man there is the swelling elevation of the mind.
And therefore before the proud Pharisees he heals the dropsical man in the body,
that by this example they might learn to be healed in spirit;
for what this man bore in his body, those men bore in their mind.
Like these Pharisees are many prelates today,
who are more anxious for the welfare of beasts than of the men committed to them.
But this covetous man stands before Jesus when he is converted to God,
ready to dispense what he has gained for the divine honor;
and so he recovers his health, for from covetousness he is changed into generosity.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, take hold of me and protect me with the hand of your mercy,
lest the dropsy of carnal pleasure, or avarice, or pride, prevail in me;
and grant me, through a true humility of heart and mouth and work,
to recline in the lowest place, reckoning myself less than others,
and through mercy not to neglect to invite the needy,
succoring their necessities according to my power.
Most bountiful Giver, O God, grant to me, a wretched man,
to eat the heavenly bread, which is you yourself, in the kingdom of God;
for which bread, O Lord, we daily do battle for you,
for which all the day long we are mortified, that we may live in your life. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Tetigit eum, non causa necessitatis, sed humilitatis; et ut divinitatem in carne ostenderet, eum sanavit.
latinJewel.english: He touched him, not from necessity, but from humility; and that he might show his divinity in the flesh, he made him whole.
